comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout with important nuance. extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/state.ts:303-311 enforces disabled-agent policy only from in-memory AgentSelectionStore state plus ralphOwnedSessionCache. isDisabledForCwd(...) returns existing?.disabledAgents.has(name) ?? false, so before activate(...) it defaults to enabled. isRalphOwnedSession(...) returns ralphOwnedSessionCache.get(cacheKey) ?? false, so Ralph policy only applies after preloadRalphOwnedSessionCache(...). AgentControl.spawn() uses isAgentDisabledForSession(...) synchronously for enforcement at extensions/tau/src/agent/control.ts:57-63. Headless session_start/session_switch paths call agentSelections.activate(...) but do not preload Ralph ownership (extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/index.ts:365-379,381-389), so Ralph-only restrictions can be bypassed there. More generally, if loadRegistrySafe(...) returns null during session startup, activate(...) is skipped while AgentControl.spawn() still performs its own AgentRegistry.load(...), leaving user disabled-agent enforcement dependent on prior cache warmup. Verified issue is that enforcement is cache-backed rather than authoritative; exact exploitability varies by path.
